source_loader_findings.finding: One GTM JavaScript loader implementation. It appends one inline bootstrap script with id gtm-loader after analytics consent.
One conditional GTM noscript iframe implementation.
source_loader_findings.duplicate_risk: none as a JavaScript analytics source

source_loader_findings.finding: One centralized dataLayer transport; no direct GA4 dispatch remains.

consent_lifecycle_findings.finding: Consent lifecycle loads GTM once; duplicate occurs inside GTM/GA4 page-view handling.
Saved consent also reproduces the duplicate without duplicate GTM loading.

page_view_source_findings.finding: PageViewTracker produced one application dataLayer page_view per tested route; it is not the duplicate source.

tested_flows.suspected_source: GTM Google Tag automatic page view plus explicit GA4 page_view event tag
GTM Google Tag automatic page view plus explicit GA4 page_view event tag

production_dom_findings.note: In JavaScript-enabled DOM, noscript iframe markup is inert. Server HTML with saved consent contains one noscript iframe.

network_findings.suspected_source: Google Tag automatic page_view plus explicit GA4 Event tag for page_view
Google Tag automatic page_view plus explicit GA4 Event tag for page_view

active_published_tags_visible.send_page_view: not set to false; automatic page_view observed

active_published_tags_visible.duplicate_risk: high when base Google Tag auto page_view is enabled

gtm_container_findings.limitations: Paused tags, draft workspace changes, tag names, and full consent settings require authenticated GTM UI/API access.

ga4_enhanced_measurement_findings.production_evidence: No Enhanced Measurement history-page-view duplicate was identified in tested SPA navigation. The reproduced duplicate occurs on initial consent-granted load and is explained by GTM category C.
ga4_enhanced_measurement_findings.manual_check_required: Enhanced Measurement -> Page views -> Page changes based on browser history events

tag_assistant_findings.gtm_preview_or_tag_assistant: not required to reproduce; authenticated Preview not inspected
tag_assistant_findings.conclusion: Preview/session-only behavior is not the primary root cause.

duplicate_categories.label: automatic Google Tag page view plus explicit page_view
evidence: One application dataLayer page_view on initial consent-granted load.
Two GA4 page_view requests with tid G-QD70DM5MEJ and dl https://www.avizsazeh.ir/.
Published GTM contains Google Tag on gtm.init and GA4 Event tag on Custom Event page_view.
send_page_view=false is not effective in the published Google Tag.

confirmed_root_cause: The published GTM Google Tag for G-QD70DM5MEJ sends an automatic initial page_view while a separate GA4 Event tag also sends the explicit application page_view event.
secondary_risks: GA4 Enhanced Measurement history-page-view setting is unverified and should be checked manually.
The app GTM loader only checks id=gtm-loader, not an existing gtm.js src or window.google_tag_manager container state; this did not cause the current duplicate but is a hardening opportunity.
SPA page_view GA4 requests were not consistently observed for route changes in the headless capture despite dataLayer page_view events; retest after fixing the initial duplicate.
recommended_fix_order: In GTM, set the Google Tag for G-QD70DM5MEJ to suppress the automatic initial page view, using send_page_view=false or the equivalent Google Tag setting.
Keep the explicit TAG - GA4 - page_view event tag triggered by the application page_view event.
Publish the GTM change and retest first consent grant and saved-consent initial load.
Verify GA4 Enhanced Measurement history-based page views are disabled if the app remains the SPA page-view source.
Recheck Tag Assistant only after normal production network count is one GA4 page_view.
